cells.speakerNotes:
- Pre-print released March 2026
  - Co-authored by Yann LeCun — one of the founding figures of modern deep learning
  - 2018 Turing Award winner (shared with Bengio & Hinton) for contributions to deep learning
  - Vocal critic of LLMs: argues autoregressive next-token prediction is the wrong path to true intelligence
  - In 2022: 60-page position paper outlining his vision for autonomous machine intelligence
  - Central idea: the Joint-Embedding Predictive Architecture (JEPA) — predict in latent space, not pixel space

- Explain action conditioning with an actual task
- T-Task: Goal to

- Activations shifts as weights update -> internal covariate shift
- Normalization layers stabilize by rescaling activations to a constant range
- In LayerNorm, only the scale and shift are learned parameters -> Same for every input that passes through
- Adaptive layer normalization keeps the math, but generates the scale and shift dynamically from a conditioning signal
- Conditioning vector a is fed through a small MLP that outputs scale and shift
- Just concatenating -> tiny action vector vs large state embedding. 
- Adding -> We want to have a multiplicative effect -> Not just scale it
